---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-15"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

################################################################################
# Framingham Heart Study - Full Project Analysis
# Outcome: Incident Stroke (10-year follow-up)
# Models: Separate Cox PH by sex
# Forced: AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES
# Candidates: BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI, PREVCHD
#
# Data location: frmgham2.csv (same directory)
################################################################################

library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
library(tableone)

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 1: DATA PREPARATION
# ==============================================================================

dat <- read.csv("frmgham2.csv")
dat$SEX <- factor(dat$SEX, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female"))

# Baseline cohort: Period 1, exclude prevalent stroke
baseline <- dat %>%
  filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0)

# 10-year outcome (3652 days)
baseline <- baseline %>%
  mutate(
    time10   = pmin(TIMESTRK, 3652),
    stroke10 = ifelse(STROKE == 1 & TIMESTRK <= 3652, 1, 0)
  )

# Cohort flow
data.frame(
  Total_Period1     = nrow(dat %>% filter(PERIOD == 1)),
  Excluded_PrevStrk = nrow(dat %>% filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 1)),
  Analytic_Cohort   = nrow(baseline)
)

# Sex-specific sample sizes and event counts
baseline %>%
  group_by(SEX) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), events = sum(stroke10), rate_pct = round(mean(stroke10)*100, 2))
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 2: MISSINGNESS
# ==============================================================================

all_vars <- c("AGE", "DIABETES", "SYSBP", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE",
              "TOTCHOL", "BMI", "PREVCHD")

baseline %>%
  select(all_of(all_vars)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "N_Missing")
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 3: TABLE 1
# ==============================================================================

table1_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI",
                 "DIABETES", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "PREVCHD")
cat_vars    <- c("DIABETES", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "PREVCHD")

# Table 1a: By Sex
CreateTableOne(
  vars = table1_vars, strata = "SEX", data = baseline,
  factorVars = cat_vars, test = TRUE
) %>% print(showAllLevels = TRUE, formatOptions = list(big.mark = ","),
            missing = TRUE)

# Table 1b: By 10-Year Stroke Status
CreateTableOne(
  vars = table1_vars, strata = "stroke10", data = baseline,
  factorVars = cat_vars, test = TRUE
) %>% print(showAllLevels = TRUE, formatOptions = list(big.mark = ","),
            missing = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 4: EXPLORATORY ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

# 4a. Continuous variable summaries
baseline %>% select(AGE, SYSBP, TOTCHOL, BMI) %>% summary()

# 4b. Skewness
sapply(c("AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI"), function(v) {
  x <- na.omit(baseline[[v]])
  round(mean(((x - mean(x)) / sd(x))^3), 3)
})

# 4c. Correlation matrix
baseline %>%
  select(AGE, SYSBP, BMI, TOTCHOL, DIABETES, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, PREVCHD) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)) %>%
  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") %>%
  round(3)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 5: KAPLAN-MEIER ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

surv_obj <- Surv(baseline$time10, baseline$stroke10)

# 5a. KM by Sex
km_sex <- survfit(surv_obj ~ SEX, data = baseline)
km_sex
summary(km_sex, times = c(365, 1826, 3652))

# Plot KM by sex
pdf("km_by_sex.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
plot(km_sex, col = c("blue", "red"), lwd = 2,
     xlab = "Time (days)", ylab = "Stroke-Free Probability",
     main = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Estimates by Sex",
     mark.time = TRUE)
legend("bottomleft", legend = c("Male", "Female"),
       col = c("blue", "red"), lwd = 2, bty = "n")
dev.off()

# Log-rank test by sex
survdiff(surv_obj ~ SEX, data = baseline)

# 5b. KM by Diabetes (overall and within sex)
km_diab <- survfit(surv_obj ~ DIABETES, data = baseline)
summary(km_diab, times = c(365, 1826, 3652))

pdf("km_by_diabetes.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
plot(km_diab, col = c("forestgreen", "darkorange"), lwd = 2,
     xlab = "Time (days)", ylab = "Stroke-Free Probability",
     main = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Estimates by Diabetes Status")
legend("bottomleft", legend = c("No Diabetes", "Diabetes"),
       col = c("forestgreen", "darkorange"), lwd = 2, bty = "n")
dev.off()

survdiff(surv_obj ~ DIABETES, data = baseline)

# 5c. KM by PREVCHD
km_chd <- survfit(surv_obj ~ PREVCHD, data = baseline)
summary(km_chd, times = c(365, 1826, 3652))
survdiff(surv_obj ~ PREVCHD, data = baseline)

# 5d. KM by BPMEDS
km_bp <- survfit(surv_obj ~ BPMEDS, data = baseline)
summary(km_bp, times = c(365, 1826, 3652))
survdiff(surv_obj ~ BPMEDS, data = baseline)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 6: COX PROPORTIONAL HAZARDS - SEPARATE BY SEX
# ==============================================================================

males   <- baseline %>% filter(SEX == "Male")
females <- baseline %>% filter(SEX == "Female")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6a. Forced-only model (AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cox_m_forced <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES,
                      data = males)
summary(cox_m_forced)

cox_f_forced <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES,
                      data = females)
summary(cox_f_forced)
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6b. Purposeful selection: add candidates one at a time
#     Retain if p < 0.15 OR removal changes forced-variable HR by >15%
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

candidates <- c("BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "TOTCHOL", "BMI", "PREVCHD")

## --- MALES ---

# Step 1: Test each candidate individually (added to forced model)
for (v in candidates) {
  f <- as.formula(paste("Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +", v))
  fit <- coxph(f, data = males)
  pval <- summary(fit)$coefficients[v, "Pr(>|z|)"]
  writeLines(sprintf("Male | + %s : p = %.4f", v, pval))
}

# Step 2: Fit full candidate model
cox_m_full <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                      BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI + PREVCHD,
                    data = males)
summary(cox_m_full)

# Step 3: Backward elimination from full model
# Remove candidate with highest p > 0.15, refit, check confounding
# Repeat until all candidates have p < 0.15 or are confounders

# Helper: percent change in forced coefficients
check_confounding <- function(fit_full, fit_reduced) {
  forced <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "DIABETES")
  coef_full <- coef(fit_full)[forced]
  coef_red  <- coef(fit_reduced)[forced]
  pct_change <- abs((coef_red - coef_full) / coef_full) * 100
  data.frame(Variable = forced, Full = round(coef_full, 5),
             Reduced = round(coef_red, 5),
             Pct_Change = round(pct_change, 1))
}

# Example: remove CURSMOKE (likely highest p), check confounding
cox_m_drop1 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                       BPMEDS + TOTCHOL + BMI + PREVCHD,
                     data = males)
writeLines("Male | Drop CURSMOKE - confounding check:")
check_confounding(cox_m_full, cox_m_drop1)

# Continue removing iteratively — adjust the formulas below based on
# the p-values from the full model output above

# [After running, fill in the final male model here]
# cox_m_final <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + ...,
#                      data = males)
# summary(cox_m_final)


```
```{r}
# --- MALE: Backward elimination with confounding checks ---

# Step 1: Drop PREVCHD (p = 0.925)
cox_m_s1 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI, data = males)
summary(cox_m_s1)
check_confounding(cox_m_full, cox_m_s1)

# Step 2: Drop BMI
cox_m_s2 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL, data = males)
summary(cox_m_s2)
check_confounding(cox_m_full, cox_m_s2)

# Step 3: Drop TOTCHOL
cox_m_s3 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE, data = males)
summary(cox_m_s3)
check_confounding(cox_m_full, cox_m_s3)

# Step 4: Drop BPMEDS
cox_m_s4 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    CURSMOKE, data = males)
summary(cox_m_s4)
check_confounding(cox_m_full, cox_m_s4)

# --- Final male model (pending confounding results) ---
cox_m_final <- cox_m_s4
summary(cox_m_final)


```

```{r}
## --- FEMALES ---

# Step 1: Test each candidate individually
for (v in candidates) {
  f <- as.formula(paste("Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +", v))
  fit <- coxph(f, data = females)
  pval <- summary(fit)$coefficients[v, "Pr(>|z|)"]
  writeLines(sprintf("Female | + %s : p = %.4f", v, pval))
}

# Step 2: Full candidate model
cox_f_full <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                      BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI + PREVCHD,
                    data = females)
summary(cox_f_full)

# Step 3: Backward elimination (same process as males)
cox_f_drop1 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                       BPMEDS + TOTCHOL + BMI + PREVCHD,
                     data = females)
writeLines("Female | Drop CURSMOKE - confounding check:")
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_drop1)

# [After running, fill in the final female model here]
# cox_f_final <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + ...,
#                      data = females)
# summary(cox_f_final)
```

```{r}
# --- FEMALE: Backward elimination ---

# Step 1: Drop BMI (p = 0.713)
cox_f_s1 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + PREVCHD, data = females)
summary(cox_f_s1)
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_s1)

# Step 2: Drop TOTCHOL (p = 0.597)
cox_f_s2 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + PREVCHD, data = females)
summary(cox_f_s2)
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_s2)

# Step 3: Drop CURSMOKE (p = 0.216)
cox_f_s3 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS + PREVCHD, data = females)
summary(cox_f_s3)
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_s3)

# Step 4: Drop PREVCHD (if p > 0.15 in step 3 model)
cox_f_s4 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES +
                    BPMEDS, data = females)
summary(cox_f_s4)
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_s4)

# Step 5: Drop BPMEDS (if p > 0.15 in step 4 model)
cox_f_s5 <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES,
                   data = females)
summary(cox_f_s5)
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_s5)


# Correct path: drop BPMEDS from step 3 (it had p = 0.17), keep PREVCHD (p = 0.13)
cox_f_candidate <- coxph(Surv(time10, stroke10) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + PREVCHD,
                         data = females)
summary(cox_f_candidate)
check_confounding(cox_f_full, cox_f_candidate)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 7: PROPORTIONAL HAZARDS DIAGNOSTICS
# ==============================================================================

# Schoenfeld residual test (run on final models; using forced model as placeholder)
cox.zph(cox_m_forced)
cox.zph(cox_f_forced)

# Schoenfeld residual plots
pdf("schoenfeld_males.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(cox.zph(cox_m_forced))
dev.off()

pdf("schoenfeld_females.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(cox.zph(cox_f_forced))
dev.off()

# Log-log survival plots by sex (visual PH check)
pdf("loglog_sex.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
plot(km_sex, fun = "cloglog", col = c("blue", "red"), lwd = 2,
     xlab = "log(Time)", ylab = "log(-log(S(t)))",
     main = "Log-Log Survival Plot by Sex")
legend("topleft", legend = c("Male", "Female"),
       col = c("blue", "red"), lwd = 2, bty = "n")
dev.off()
```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 8: 10-YEAR STROKE PROBABILITY ESTIMATES
# ==============================================================================

# Define risk profiles (adjust model formula to match final model)
# Using forced model as placeholder — update to final model after selection

# Low risk: age 40, SBP 120, no diabetes
# Moderate risk: age 55, SBP 140, no diabetes
# High risk: age 65, SBP 160, diabetes

risk_profiles <- data.frame(
  AGE      = c(40, 55, 65),
  SYSBP    = c(120, 140, 160),
  DIABETES = c(0, 1, 1),
  Profile  = c("Low Risk", "Moderate Risk", "High Risk")
)

# Male predictions
surv_pred_m <- survfit(cox_m_forced,
                       newdata = risk_profiles %>% select(-Profile))
# Extract 10-year (3652-day) survival probabilities
surv_summ_m <- summary(surv_pred_m, times = 3652)
data.frame(
  Profile     = risk_profiles$Profile,
  Sex         = "Male",
  Surv_10yr   = round(surv_summ_m$surv, 4),
  Stroke_Prob = round(1 - surv_summ_m$surv, 4)
)

# Female predictions
surv_pred_f <- survfit(cox_f_forced,
                       newdata = risk_profiles %>% select(-Profile))
surv_summ_f <- summary(surv_pred_f, times = 3652)
data.frame(
  Profile     = risk_profiles$Profile,
  Sex         = "Female",
  Surv_10yr   = round(surv_summ_f$surv, 4),
  Stroke_Prob = round(1 - surv_summ_f$surv, 4)
)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 9: TIME-VARYING COVARIATE DESCRIPTIVE ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

dat_all3 <- dat %>%
  group_by(RANDID) %>%
  filter(n() == 3) %>%
  ungroup()

n_distinct(dat_all3$RANDID)

# 9a. SYSBP by period
dat_all3 %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), mean = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd = sd(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE), median = median(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE))

# 9b. SYSBP change: P1->P2, P2->P3, and P1->P3
wide_sysbp <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, SYSBP) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = SYSBP, names_prefix = "P")

wide_sysbp <- wide_sysbp %>%
  mutate(delta_12 = P2 - P1,
         delta_23 = P3 - P2,
         delta_13 = P3 - P1)

writeLines("SYSBP Change: Period 1 -> Period 2")
summary(wide_sysbp$delta_12)

writeLines("SYSBP Change: Period 2 -> Period 3")
summary(wide_sysbp$delta_23)

writeLines("SYSBP Change: Period 1 -> Period 3")
summary(wide_sysbp$delta_13)

# 9c. Diabetes prevalence by period
dat_all3 %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), n_diabetes = sum(DIABETES, na.rm = TRUE),
            pct = mean(DIABETES, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)

# 9d. Diabetes transitions: P1->P2, P2->P3, P1->P3
wide_diab <- dat_all3 %>%
  select(RANDID, PERIOD, DIABETES) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = DIABETES, names_prefix = "P")

writeLines("Diabetes Transition: Period 1 -> Period 2")
table(wide_diab$P1, wide_diab$P2, dnn = c("Period1", "Period2"))

writeLines("Diabetes Transition: Period 2 -> Period 3")
table(wide_diab$P2, wide_diab$P3, dnn = c("Period2", "Period3"))

writeLines("Diabetes Transition: Period 1 -> Period 3")
table(wide_diab$P1, wide_diab$P3, dnn = c("Period1", "Period3"))

```
